filesystem.c(3) Library Functions Manual filesystem.c(3)

NAME

filesystem.c - Cross-platform filesystem utilities and binary PATH cache.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct bin_cache_entry_t
Cache entry for binary PATH lookup.

Functions


const char * file_read_error_message (const char *path)
const char * file_write_error_message (const char *path)
void platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache (void)
bool platform_is_binary_in_path (const char *bin_name)
bool platform_get_executable_path (char *exe_path, size_t path_size)
Get the path to the current executable.

Detailed Description

Cross-platform filesystem utilities and binary PATH cache.

Definition in file filesystem.c.

Function Documentation

const char * file_read_error_message (const char * path)

Definition at line 20 of file filesystem.c.

20                                                       {
21   switch (errno) {
22   case ENOENT:
23     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "File does not exist: %s", path);
24     break;
25   case EACCES:
26     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Permission denied (cannot read): %s", path);
27     break;
28   case EISDIR:
29     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Is a directory, not a file: %s", path);
30     break;
31   default:
32     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Failed to open for reading: %s (%s)", path,
33              platform_strerror(errno));
34     break;
35   }
36   return error_msg_buffer;
37 }




References platform_strerror().


Referenced by update_check_load_cache().






const
  char * file_write_error_message (const char * path)


Definition at line 39 of file filesystem.c.


39                                                        {
40   switch (errno) {
41   case ENOENT:
42     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Directory does not exist: %s", path);
43     break;
44   case EACCES:
45     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Permission denied (cannot write): %s", path);
46     break;
47   case EROFS:
48     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Read-only filesystem: %s", path);
49     break;
50   case ENOSPC:
51     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "No space left on device: %s", path);
52     break;
53   case EISDIR:
54     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Is a directory, not a file: %s", path);
55     break;
56   default:
57     snprintf(error_msg_buffer, sizeof(error_msg_buffer), "Failed to open for writing: %s (%s)", path,
58              platform_strerror(errno));
59     break;
60   }
61   return error_msg_buffer;
62 }






References platform_strerror().


Referenced by update_check_save_cache().






void
  platform_cleanup_binary_path_cache (void )


Definition at line 109 of file filesystem.c.


109                                               {
110   if (!lifecycle_shutdown(&g_cache_lc)) {
111     return;
112   }
113 
114   if (g_bin_path_cache) {
115     rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
116 
117     bin_cache_entry_t *entry, *tmp;
118     HASH_ITER(hh, g_bin_path_cache, entry, tmp) {
119       HASH_DELETE(hh, g_bin_path_cache, entry);
120       free_cache_entry(entry);
121     }
122 
123     rwlock_wrunlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
124     rwlock_destroy(&g_cache_rwlock);
125     g_bin_path_cache = NULL;
126   }
127 }






References lifecycle_shutdown().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






bool platform_get_executable_path (char * exe_path, size_t
  path_size)


Get the path to the current executable.


Parameters


exe_path Buffer to store the executable path


path_size Size of the buffer


Returns


true on success, false on failure


Definition at line 185 of file filesystem.c.


185                                                                     {
186   if (!exe_path || path_size == 0) {
187     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters: exe_path=%p, path_size=%zu", (void *)exe_path, path_size);
188     return false;
189   }
190 
191 #ifdef _WIN32
192   DWORD len = GetModuleFileNameA(NULL, exe_path, (DWORD)path_size);
193   if (len == 0) {
194     SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "GetModuleFileNameA failed: error code %lu", GetLastError());
195     return false;
196   }
197   if (len >= path_size) {
198     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW,
199               "Executable path exceeds buffer size (path length >= %zu bytes, buffer size = %zu bytes)", (size_t)len,
200               path_size);
201     return false;
202   }
203   return true;
204 
205 #elif defined(__linux__)
206   ssize_t len = readlink("/proc/self/exe", exe_path, path_size - 1);
207   if (len < 0) {
208     SET_ERRNO_SYS(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "readlink(\"/proc/self/exe\") failed: %s", SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
209     return false;
210   }
211   if ((size_t)len >= path_size - 1) {
212     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW,
213               "Executable path exceeds buffer size (path length >= %zu bytes, buffer size = %zu bytes)", (size_t)len,
214               path_size);
215     return false;
216   }
217   exe_path[len] = '\0';
218   return true;
219 
220 #elif defined(__APPLE__)
221   uint32_t bufsize = (uint32_t)path_size;
222   int result = _NSGetExecutablePath(exe_path, &bufsize);
223   if (result != 0) {
224     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_BUFFER_OVERFLOW, "_NSGetExecutablePath failed: path requires %u bytes, buffer size = %zu bytes",
225               bufsize, path_size);
226     return false;
227   }
228   return true;
229 
230 #else
231   SET_ERRNO(ERROR_GENERAL, "Unsupported platform - cannot get executable path");
232   return false;
233 #endif
234 }






References SET_ERRNO.






bool platform_is_binary_in_path (const char *
  bin_name)


Definition at line 129 of file filesystem.c.


129                                                       {
130   if (!bin_name || bin_name[0] == '\0') {
131     return false;
132   }
133 
134   init_cache_once();
135   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_cache_lc)) {
136     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Binary PATH cache not initialized, checking directly (this should never happen)");
137     return check_binary_in_path_uncached(bin_name);
138   }
139 
140   rwlock_rdlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
141   bin_cache_entry_t *entry = NULL;
142   HASH_FIND_STR(g_bin_path_cache, bin_name, entry);
143   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
144 
145   if (entry) {
146     log_dev("Binary '%s' %s in PATH (%s)", bin_name, colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, "found"),
147             colored_string(LOG_COLOR_WARN, "cached"));
148     return entry->in_path;
149   }
150 
151   bool found = check_binary_in_path_uncached(bin_name);
152 
153   entry = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(bin_cache_entry_t), bin_cache_entry_t *);
154   if (!entry) {
155     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate cache entry");
156     return found;
157   }
158 
159   entry->bin_name = platform_strdup(bin_name);
160   if (!entry->bin_name) {
161     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to duplicate binary name");
162     SAFE_FREE(entry);
163     return found;
164   }
165 
166   entry->in_path = found;
167 
168   rwlock_wrlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
169   HASH_ADD_KEYPTR(hh, g_bin_path_cache, entry->bin_name, strlen(entry->bin_name), entry);
170   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_cache_rwlock);
171 
172   char *found_notfound_str =
173       found ? colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, "found") : colored_string(LOG_COLOR_ERROR, "NOT found");
174   log_dev("Binary '%s' %s in PATH", bin_name, found_notfound_str);
175 
176   return found;
177 }






References bin_cache_entry_t::bin_name,
    check_binary_in_path_uncached(), colored_string(),
    bin_cache_entry_t::in_path, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    platform_strdup(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC, and
    SET_ERRNO.
